$AMZN AWS launched Claude Mythos in gated preview through Bedrock for advanced cybersecurity and code analysis.

Access is limited to allowlisted organizations under Project Glasswing and AWS also introduced an Agent Registry for managing and reusing AI agents + tools.

$NVDA has reportedly spent more than a year exploring a deal for a large PC-focused company that it believes could reshape the PC and server landscape.

$DELL jumping 7% on the article.

$INTC IS HAVING ITS BEST WEEK SINCE 2000

Goldman Sachs maintained its Buy on $NBIS and raised its price target to $205 citing the company’s $27B AI infrastructure deal with $META.

The firm lifted FY27 to FY30 revenue estimates by ~45% and EBITDA by ~50% on the Meta ramp.

$MSFT is developing new Copilot features inspired by OpenClaw including always-on AI agents that can work across Microsoft 365 apps around the clock.

That would push Copilot further toward a true cross-app agent.

$SOFI is enabling 24/7 instant payments through FedNow via Galileo allowing users to both send and receive money in seconds.

Most banks only support incoming instant payments but SoFi is offering both sides.

OpenAI is reportedly leaning further into $AMZN for enterprise distribution with its CRO saying $MSFT has “limited our ability” to meet customers where they are.

She added that demand through Amazon has been “staggering” as OpenAI looks to broaden enterprise reach beyond Azure.

$ONDS secured an initial ~$68M order under a broader $140M military program marking the first phase of a multi-year defense award.

The vehicles are being built for AI & autonomous integration with deliveries set to begin in Q4 2026.

$ASTS BlueBird 7 is set to launch on Blue Origin’s New Glenn which went vertical at LC-36 overnight ahead of the mission.

If the launch happens in the coming days then it would mark Blue Origin’s third launch and the first reflight of a New Glenn booster.

$META is on track to overtake $GOOGL as the world’s largest digital advertising business this year.

A pretty wild shift considering Google has dominated online ads for years.

$AMZN has expanded Amazon Autos beyond Hyundai to include Kia, Mazda, Subaru, Chevrolet and Jeep.

The online new-car platform is now live in more than 130 U.S. cities as Amazon pushes deeper into digital auto sales.
Bitcoin defending $70K all weekend is a solid signal that risk appetite is still there.

Private credit was always on a search for companies that had a big moat (meaning competitors could not easily disrupt them) and stable cash flows.

For years companies that fit this criteria were Software/SaaS companies. There are tens of thousands of them that makes specialized software for every industry and a multitude of applications. So private credit funds have a big weighting in Software/SaaS companies.

Then AI came and disrupted the Software/SaaS industry. Now the cost of making software is collapsing towards zero and the fear is these companies are going to get disrupted by low cost entrants (smaller/no moat) offering the same Software/SaaS products at a fraction of the price (uncertain cash flows).

That said, Software/SaaS companies haven’t been disrupted yet. So when the public companies release  their Q1 earnings reports, they will show their moats and cash flows are still very much intact.

But this is an issue of where the Software/SaaS companies are going next, not where they are now.

Publicly traded Software/SaaS stocks have been getting crushed in the last few months on fears that these companies are about to experience huge disruption thanks to AI.

The private credit funds are in a difficult spot. They value these companies based on where they are now, not necessarily an expectation of where they’re going next. 

Investors in private credit funds think these companies are in trouble, so if private credit funds CORRECTLY say that their valuation RIGHT NOW is still top dollar (because it is), then private credit investors will take that valuation and run for the hills. This is why these funds are seeing huge requests for redemption.

It’s a difficult thing to do, which is why they have a problem in simultaneously telling everyone that the cash flows of these companies are great NOW, and also convinced them that they will be great going FORWARD in a world of abundant AI.

Before the war, the world was producing about 100M barrels of oil a day, and consuming about 100M barrels of oil a day.

Now the world is producing somewhere between 85M and 90M barrels of oil a day. That means somebody has to stop consuming 10M to 15M barrels of oil a day. Economists call this demand destruction.

The market has a very efficient way to get to this reduced level of demand, it raises the price forcing marginal buyers to stop consuming oil.

But if government subsidize the current high price of oil, the demand does not change and the market price has to go higher in order to get that demand destruction.

I’m all in favor of lower oil prices like everybody else, but we’ve got a supply demand imbalance that needs to be brought into equilibrium. 

So either open the Strait so we can get back to 100M of oil a day, or figure out another way to tell the equivalent of 10m to 15M barrels of oil a day that they cannot go to the gas station to fill up their car.

Lots of posts in my feed crowing that empty tankers are sailing to the US for crude oil. I guess they forgot that this means demand for US crude and higher prices.

Go to your local gas station and see if you can spark a "U-S-A" chant at the pumps.

Let us know how it goes.

Yes, it appears supertankers are heading to the US to get oil.

The US exports 5 million barrels a day. So, the US has it.

BUT....

The majority of Middle Eastern crude oil goes to Asia. That is a 21-day trip from the Persian Gulf versus a 50+ day trip from the United States. (A fully laden supertanker tops out at 14 to 17 MPH)

So, if the world is going to get its crude from the US, it needs twice as many oil tankers.

Expanding the fleet is a 3- to 5-year process.
The US negotiators have departed Pakistan after talks with Iran fell apart.

📺 NOT ALL PULLBACKS ARE BEARISH

Despite failed peace talks over the weekend, the market reaction so far today is muted and constructive (“in stride”), not panic-driven.

After a sharp two-week rally (~10% off lows), a pullback is healthy, expected, and even desired by traders to reset positioning and reload.

This is not a bearish breakdown—it’s a controlled digestion phase, which is good.

So, the market is absorbing negative headlines well, which is typically bullish behavior under the surface.

*

After a strong move last week, many traders sold into strength (trimmed longs near highs), while some added hedges or short exposure due to overbought conditions (oscillators moved from +45 → +60, which is overextended).

I still hold a short $SPY hedge to absorb downside and create flexibility to buy into weakness. I also sold options premium.

So, I would not rush early today, let the first hour bring price discovery, and then gradually reduce hedges and add to strong names.

*

Key Levels to Watch Today $SPY

These are your decision zones: 
– ~$673.50–$675 → Key hold level 
– ~$671.50 → Gap support zone
– ~$673 (Thursday low) → Still holding above

Right now, $SPY is at ~$675 → above key support, not even testing the gap yet → market still strong.

Bullish Case:
Hold above ~$673.50, gap stays intact, moving averages catch up, and leadership shows relative strength.

As a result, you get a clean continuation setup and strong stocks outperform.

Neutral / Reset Case:
Pullback into ~$671.50 gap, consolidation + chop, sscillators reset.

As a result, slower but still constructive market.

Bearish Case (less likely for now):
Gap fills aggressively, lose key levels, then reassess where buyers step in.

*

“Blood in the Street” Update

To remind you, this account is designed for extreme oversold entries. I have only deployed one tier, so no trimming yet. 

Normally, after a sharp bounce → I would execute partial profit-taking, but here I would let it ride due to limited exposure.

Important nuance: this isn’t blind conviction—it’s position sizing discipline.

*

So, the market had a powerful rally for ~10 days. Now it’s cooling off, not breaking down. 

Manage your exposure and look to rebuy leaders.

If key levels hold → bullish trend still intact.
